guest: Iman Bouzade, MD, CEO and Co-Founder of Incredible Health

business_ideas.idea: Marketplace platform to hire permanent nurses in less than 30 days vs 90+ days
Other operational healthcare backend stack innovations (compliance, revenue cycle, staffing)

predictions.text: Healthcare operational stack will see major innovation similar to patient-facing solutions
Proper staffing levels reduce medication errors, readmissions, and patient mortality rates

disagreements: Shaan believes most paths to entrepreneurship require either business school pedigree or direct startup experience; Iman argues MBA is one of many valid paths, not essential
dynamics: Iman grounds with medical/operational expertise while Shaan drives philosophical debate on education and paths to success. Strong collaborative energy as both validate each other's frameworks.
frameworks: Four criteria for good ideas (hair-on-fire problem, huge market, 10x better solution, ideal competitor count)
Three optimization modes (cash, skills/learning, lifestyle) at different career stages
learnings: Pivoting during accelerator with demo-day deadline is stressful but viable with good advisors
Medical family background can both constrain choices and provide unique domain insight
Warm introductions from respected mentors dramatically accelerate funding and fundraising success

banger_quotes.quote: There has to be a better way
I went straight into problem solving mode

numbers_dropped: 2M seed round took 75-100 meetings to raise
$50M Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz
notable: Iman's pivot story from failed product to 50M Series A in 2 years exemplifies iterative founder resilience.
